Container.Name: PortOS-Postgres
Container.Overview: PostgreSQL + pgvector backend for a networked PortOS install. Each PortOS install normally runs its own local Docker/native PostgreSQL, but a machine can instead point at this container over Tailscale (PGMODE=network) so a home server (e.g. Unraid) hosts the database for one or more PortOS machines. Requires the pgvector extension for PortOS's creative-catalog vector search; do not swap in a plain postgres image. Do not expose this container to the public internet.
Container.Requires: Only reachable over Tailscale/LAN, never the public internet. The PortOS `.env` on each connecting machine needs PGMODE=network plus matching PGHOST/PGPORT/PGDATABASE/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD; see the repository setup guide.

Config.Name: Host Port
Config.Description: PostgreSQL protocol port. Point each PortOS machine's PGHOST/PGPORT at this over Tailscale/LAN. Never forward this port to the public internet.

Config.Name: Postgres Database
Config.Description: Database name. Must match PGDATABASE in every connecting PortOS install's .env.

Config.Name: Postgres User
Config.Description: Database role. Must match PGUSER in every connecting PortOS install's .env.

Config.Name: Postgres Password
Config.Description: Database password. Choose a real value here and match it to PGPASSWORD in every connecting PortOS install's .env — do not reuse the local-dev default of 'portos'.

Config.Name: Postgres Data
Config.Description: Persistent PostgreSQL data directory. Back this up like any other stateful appdata.
